mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals impending corporate revenue contraction, triggering a repricing of equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances coinciding with persistent inflation indicate severe purchasing power erosion, which structurally reduces aggregate demand and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value > 0.10 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained macroeconomic environment, rising industrial production demonstrates firm pricing power and margin expansion, directly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases correlate with negative or flat 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal's information coefficient falls below 0.02 during supply bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.4 million units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, which transmits to broader equity markets and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances following a housing starts drop below 1.4M, or the signal exhibits no statistically significant predictive power (p > 0.05) over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding a 2.5% annualized rate sustains corporate earnings momentum and validates equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous elevated federal funds rates and sticky consumer price inflation compress real yields and equity risk premiums, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value > 0.10 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, strong payroll growth combined with stable unemployment confirms economic resilience, boosting short-term equity momentum and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: Payroll growth and stable unemployment correlate with negative 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.
